C-WORKFLOW-RESULT-PRESERVATION.IntentSummary: Implement the approved workflow result preservation change by keeping strict tracked-result protocol semantics while carrying bounded subagent result context through parsing, state storage, work_item_list recovery, hard-stop errors, protocol errors, and format-only repair diagnostics.

Baseline.Note: M-WORKFLOW-PROTOCOL currently parses strict top-block metadata but does not expose body text from valid tracked results.
M-PLUGIN-WORKFLOW currently throws RESULT_PROTOCOL_ERROR and RESULT_HARD_STOP messages that omit original subagent result context.
M-WORKFLOW-STATE currently persists work-item status and review-round metadata without result body excerpts.
M-WORKFLOW-TOOLING currently serializes work_item_list without recovery excerpts for blocked or needs_context states.
M-WORKFLOW-REPAIR currently receives malformed output but does not have a dedicated missing-blank-line diagnostic path.
src/plugins/workflow.test.ts is the existing deterministic verification surface for protocol, repair, state, tooling, plugin integration, and persistence behavior.

Target.Note: Valid tracked results expose body text for bounded excerpt creation without weakening strict top-block parsing.
Malformed top blocks with body text before the required blank line fail closed with a missing-blank-line diagnostic and corrected format guidance.
Protocol failures, state-application failures, BLOCKED, and NEEDS_CONTEXT hard stops include capped original result context in controller-visible errors.
work_item_list exposes bounded result excerpts for blocked and needs_context recovery, including implementer and reviewer contexts.
Excerpt storage is deterministic, capped, and explicit about truncation.

T-001.Title: Extend workflow protocol parsing with result body and actionable missing-blank-line diagnostics.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: ParsedResultBlock includes the freeform result body that follows the blank line after the strict protocol top block.
The parser continues to require a blank line before any body text and does not silently accept malformed header-body separation.
A new stable protocol error code or equivalent diagnostic identifies body text inside the strict top block as a missing blank line before body.
The diagnostic includes the offending line and a corrected example with VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID, VVOC_STATUS, optional VVOC_ROUTE, a blank line, then body text.
Existing duplicate-field, unknown-status, disallowed-status, work-item mismatch, and missing-route failures remain deterministic.
Update MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, and CHANGE_SUMMARY in protocol.ts to describe body extraction and the new diagnostic.

T-002.Title: Add deterministic bounded result excerpt handling to workflow result data.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Introduce a small workflow-owned result excerpt shape that contains text, truncation metadata, and enough source metadata to explain whether the excerpt came from parsed body text or normalized output.
Excerpt creation is deterministic and enforces a single explicit maximum character limit.
Valid parsed result bodies are preferred for hard-stop recovery excerpts; parse failures can fall back to normalized task output.
Excerpt text is omitted or marked empty when the subagent result has no usable body/output text rather than storing undefined behavior.
No unbounded raw subagent output is added to persisted workflow state.

T-003.Title: Persist and expose bounded recovery excerpts in workflow state and work_item_list.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: WorkItemRecord can carry the latest relevant implementer result excerpt for blocked or needs_context recovery.
ReviewRoundResult can carry the reviewer result excerpt, especially for reviewer NEEDS_CONTEXT recovery.
applyTrackedResult stores excerpts without changing allowed statuses, lifecycle states, reviewer aggregation, counters, or round-limit semantics.
work_item_list serialization exposes the bounded excerpt fields in a stable, JSON-friendly shape.
Workflow persistence hydrates and snapshots optional excerpt fields while rejecting corrupt or structurally invalid persisted records as before.
Update MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, and CHANGE_SUMMARY in state.ts, tooling.ts, and persistence.ts where scope or exported shapes change.

T-004.Title: Propagate result excerpts through workflow plugin protocol errors and hard stops.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: When parsing fails after optional repair, RESULT_PROTOCOL_ERROR includes the protocol error plus a capped excerpt of the original normalized tracked task output.
When state application rejects a parsed result, RESULT_PROTOCOL_ERROR includes the state error plus a capped excerpt of the parsed body or normalized output.
When a result transitions the work item to needs_context or blocked, RESULT_HARD_STOP includes work item id, agent, status, and the bounded result excerpt.
Hard-stop state is still snapshotted before throwing, preserving current fail-closed behavior.
Logs do not need to include full excerpts; controller-visible thrown errors and work_item_list are the required recovery surfaces.
Update MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, and CHANGE_SUMMARY in index.ts for result context propagation.

T-005.Title: Improve format-only repair guidance for missing blank lines before result bodies.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Repair prompts receive enough protocol-error detail to identify the missing-blank-line case.
When the missing-blank-line diagnostic is present, the repair prompt explicitly instructs moving body text below a blank line after the protocol header.
Repair prompts continue to forbid additional implementation or review work and require preserving the same work item, status, route, and underlying outcome.
Existing safe repair eligibility remains bounded and does not include ambiguous duplicate-field outputs.
Update MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, and CHANGE_SUMMARY in repair.ts.

T-006.Title: Strengthen workflow regression tests for excerpts, diagnostics, repair guidance, and persistence.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Tests cover valid BLOCKED and NEEDS_CONTEXT results whose thrown hard-stop errors include original result excerpts.
Tests cover work_item_list exposing implementer and reviewer recovery excerpts after blocked or needs_context states.
Tests cover malformed output after repair failure where RESULT_PROTOCOL_ERROR includes an original normalized output excerpt.
Tests cover state-application failures, such as duplicate reviewer result or reviewer not in flight, preserving a parsed result excerpt in the error.
Tests cover missing blank line before Findings/body text and assert the actionable diagnostic rather than the generic non-protocol line message.
Tests cover repair prompt text for the missing-blank-line case.
Tests cover excerpt truncation metadata and persistence round-trip of optional excerpt fields.
Update workflow.test.ts MODULE_MAP and CHANGE_SUMMARY for new test surfaces.

T-007.Title: Update GRACE graph and verification artifacts for changed workflow module scope.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: .grace/graph/main.xml reflects any changed workflow module purposes, dependencies, paths, or data-flow descriptions introduced by result body/excerpt handling.
.grace/verification/main.xml reflects strengthened verification intent for result parsing, hard stops, work_item_list recovery excerpts, and repair diagnostics.
No graph or verification anchors are renamed unless the implementation introduces an intentional new module, which this plan does not require.

T-008.Title: Run final workflow-focused and repository quality verification.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Workflow tests pass with the new result preservation coverage.
Type checking, lint, format check, build, and GRACE validation pass or any skipped check is explicitly reported with reason.
The implementation remains within the approved scope and does not make the parser forgiving.
